title: Shepherds of the Thunderwild
tagline: Young storm-herders guide the last thunderbeasts across a continent losing its weather.

cast.name: Asha Neral
cast.role: Young storm-herder leading her first solo migration
cast.visualDescription: Lean young woman with deep brown skin, large dark eyes and wind-tossed black curls tied high with copper rings, layered cobalt-and-teal riding wraps over weathered brown leathers, insulated forearm bands and high prairie boots; carries a forked storm-bell staff and torn green-blue shepherd banner for a tall unmistakable silhouette.

cast.name: Boro Tem
cast.role: Veteran shepherd who teaches Asha to hear the ground-song
cast.visualDescription: Broad weathered older man with dark umber skin, close iron-gray braids and a square beard, heavy navy herder coat with a pale storm-wool collar, vibration plates strapped across both palms and a low resonant bell at the belt; grounded crouching silhouette built around one hand touching the earth.

cast.name: Tavi Rook
cast.role: Former poacher guiding the herd through the barriers he helped build
cast.visualDescription: Long-limbed young man with tawny-brown skin, shaved sides and a loose black topknot, charcoal cable-cutter coat patched with ochre and teal, climbing harness and coils of deactivated tag wire; angular guarded silhouette with oversized insulated shears carried low rather than raised as a weapon.

storefront.tileSubtitle: Guide the last great storm herd home
storefront.keyArtPrompt: A sweeping family fantasy adventure illustration of anxious young storm-herder Asha Neral riding across prairie beneath colossal thunderbeasts with translucent branching horns, cobalt storm sky, silver hides, hot-pink lightning, prairie green, wind-torn shepherd banners and curtains of rain; tiny riders emphasize the herd's immense gentle scale, hopeful motion toward a distant calving range, no text, no logos, portrait composition with a bold creature silhouette.

prologueFrames.title: The Sky Forgets to Answer
paragraphs: Rain once followed the thunderbeasts from horizon to horizon, waking rivers and seedbeds wherever their hooves charged the earth. Now fences cut the old routes and cities draw lightning from herds that never return.
Asha Neral reads every sign of a gathering storm, but on the morning of her first solo migration, the promised clouds dissolve over fields already cracking with heat.
prologueFrames.semanticLabel: Asha waits on a dry green-brown prairie while enormous thunderbeasts face an empty cobalt sky.

prologueFrames.title: When the Herd Breaks
paragraphs: The thirsty herd chases the vanishing clouds and scatters against a new line of humming fences. Calves call from the dust while frightened adults carry their storms in eight different directions.
Asha knows the weather better than anyone her age, yet knowledge feels small when every creature is waiting for her to choose which life to reach first.
prologueFrames.semanticLabel: Thunderbeasts scatter between humming fences as lightning forks through dust around Asha.

prologueFrames.title: Listen Below the Storm
paragraphs: Veteran shepherd Boro Tem places Asha's palm against the ground. Through the trembling soil, she feels the herd speaking beneath the noise of wind and fear.
The message carries an old destination: a calving ground beyond the fenced continent, on a route no surviving map records. Asha lifts the shepherd's banner and answers through the earth.
prologueFrames.semanticLabel: Boro and Asha kneel with hands on luminous vibrating soil as distant herd calls ripple toward them.

chapters.title: The Storm That Never Came
chapters.caption: Gather a herd that followed rain into nothing.
paragraphs: Asha follows static prints through the dust and arranges resonant shepherd bells to call scattered families away from the dead cloud bank. Each recovered beast adds its pulse to a growing map beneath her feet.

The largest straggler, Forkhorn Staticback, is trapped inside a loop of its own frightened lightning and charges every approaching rider. Asha mirrors its heartbeat through the bells until the antlered giant recognizes the herd's answering rhythm.
chapters.semanticLabel: Asha rings storm bells before an enormous fork-horned thunderbeast encircled by frightened lightning.

encounters.name: Loopspark Ibex
encounters.description: A frightened silver ibex with backward glass fork horns, oversized climbing hooves and a looping pink charge tethered from horn to tail like a lasso.

encounters.name: Rainclapper Petrel
encounters.description: A long-winged cobalt prairie petrel with a hollow brass breast that rings false herd calls, trailing a narrow rain curtain beneath its wedge-shaped tail.

chapters.title: The Language Below
chapters.caption: Learn the ground-song that holds the herd together.
paragraphs: Boro teaches Asha to separate hoof-fall, warning, kin-call, and route-memory in the vibrations below them. Together they relay a message across buried stone and locate calves stranded beyond the fences.

Decades of stolen charge have awakened The Lightning Root beneath the fence line, and its luminous branches scramble every ground-signal they touch. Asha retunes the herd's steps into one patient cadence, soothing the root long enough for the calves to cross.
chapters.semanticLabel: Luminous roots split the prairie beneath Boro, Asha, and a line of thunderbeast calves.

encounters.name: Signalroot Centipede
encounters.description: A long prairie-sod centipede with dozens of luminous yellow root legs and a blind stone head, braiding every hoof-message beneath its body into static.

encounters.name: Cadence Cicada
encounters.description: A turquoise crystal cicada with a metronome abdomen and transparent waveform wings, pulsing the right ground-song one beat too early until the herd loses step.

chapters.title: The Poacher's Path
chapters.caption: Take the hidden route without repeating its harm.
paragraphs: Former poacher Tavi Rook offers a passage around the new barriers, reading broken grass and cut wire faster than any map. Old trap marks along the route make Asha wonder whether he is guiding the herd to safety or back to his buyers.

A hunter's scouting creature called The Snarewing Kite dives with cable feathers meant to tag the matriarchs. Tavi gives up the codes that once protected his livelihood, letting Asha ground it safely and proving which future he has chosen.
chapters.semanticLabel: Tavi cuts signal cables from a metallic kite creature diving above Asha and the migrating herd.

encounters.name: Tagwire Mantis
encounters.description: A tall copper tracking mantis on spool-shaped joints, with barcode antennae and cable-launching forearms that knot bright license tags around thunderbeast footprints.

encounters.name: Beacon-Barb Swallow
encounters.description: A tiny fast swallow armored in black signal foil, its needle beak and detachable glowing tail feathers designed to mark matriarchs for the hunter circling above.

chapters.title: Many Tempers, One Herd
chapters.caption: Move as one without forcing every creature alike.
paragraphs: Heat-blasted farms leave only a narrow passage between fragile crops and a dry ravine. Asha pairs bold scouts, cautious calves, and solitary elders with different signals so cooperation does not become obedience.

At midday, The Thirst Hollow opens in the ravine and pulls moisture, sound, and charge toward its lightless center. The herd escapes when Asha lets each family answer the hazard in its own way, combining their different strengths into a rain-bearing front.
chapters.semanticLabel: Different thunderbeast families form a protective arc around a dark drought hollow in scorched farmland.

encounters.name: Dewdrinker Wombat
encounters.description: A low dust-colored wombat with a cracked black cistern sunk into its broad back, drawing beads of water and sound into the dry opening with each slow breath.

encounters.name: Silencedust Funnel
encounters.description: A walking inverted dust devil balanced on three pebble feet, with bands of stolen moisture orbiting a lightless throat and different animal calls trapped between its rings.

chapters.title: The Silent Wind Valley
chapters.caption: Rebuild a route where compass, cloud, and echo fail.
paragraphs: Inside the silent valley, no breeze carries scent, no compass keeps north, and the ground returns no herd-call. The shepherds reconstruct the forgotten migration from worn hoof basins, mineral traces, and Boro's oldest remembered rhythm.

Heat trapped for generations rises as The Heatglass Behemoth, sealing each landmark behind walls of molten mirage. Asha guides the thunderbeasts to release their charge in measured waves, cooling a real path instead of chasing reflections.
chapters.semanticLabel: A molten glass giant rises between mirrored cliffs as measured lightning cools a path through the silent valley.

encounters.name: Heatglass Jerboa
encounters.description: A long-legged obsidian jerboa with sail-like heatglass ears reflecting a false valley exit, orange furnace seams opening across its body whenever anyone follows the reflection.

encounters.name: Prismwing Locust
encounters.description: A long pale locust with mirrored cliff-slab wings and sun-hot joints, multiplying into rainbow afterimages that land several body lengths from the real insect.

chapters.title: The City of Borrowed Lightning
chapters.caption: Free the matriarch without darkening a million homes.
paragraphs: The lost route ends beneath Director Sen's metropolis, where a captive matriarch powers pumps, clinics, cooling halls, and whole neighborhoods. Sen does not deny the cruelty; she asks Asha which lives should lose power the moment the chains come off.

The Loadkeeper Array locks every circuit to the matriarch's heartbeat and treats rescue as a fatal outage. Asha, Boro, and Sen reroute essential loads through stored storm cells, gaining one night to design a freedom the city can survive.
chapters.semanticLabel: A captive thunderbeast matriarch glows inside a vast city generator while Asha and Sen reroute brass circuits.

encounters.name: Pulseplug Anemone
encounters.description: A hand-sized brass wall anemone with six plug-tipped arms and a glowing medicine vial at its center, reflexively clamping any circuit that separates from the matriarch's pulse.

encounters.name: Breakerback Ox
encounters.description: A square industrial ox built from ceramic switchgear, carrying a row of city breakers along its spine and locking its broad copper horns across every alternate route.

chapters.title: A Grid That Can Migrate
chapters.caption: Build a power system that follows instead of cages.
paragraphs: Asha proposes seasonal relay towers that gather passing charge and return migration corridors to the herd. Citizens, shepherds, and skeptical engineers must align a moving grid before the city's reserves fail and panic closes the gates.

Every failed circuit leaves behind The Brownout Choir, spectral echoes that repeat the city's worst memories of cold and darkness until no one will risk another change. Boro turns the echoes into a shared pulse, and the citizens complete the relay by following vibration when the lights go out.
chapters.semanticLabel: Citizens raise portable relay towers through ghostly waves of blackout fear as Boro marks a rhythm on the ground.

encounters.name: Blackout Veil
encounters.description: A tall floating curtain of midnight cloth with apartment windows blinking across its folds, wrapping relay workers in remembered rooms where the lights never returned.

encounters.name: Coldroom Echo
encounters.description: A hollow hunched figure made from blankets, visible breath and extinguished lamp chimneys, with a dark doorway in its chest repeating one family's worst outage.

chapters.title: The Great Shared Migration
chapters.caption: Carry herd, city, and weather into one living cycle.
paragraphs: The matriarch walks free as mobile relays, citizen caravans, and the reunited herd cross the final range together. At the lost calving ground, Asha must place every tower and thunderbeast within the ancient pattern before the continent's divided weather collapses.

The gathering fronts become The Skywheel Colossus, a continent-wide storm whose turning bands can nourish every watershed or tear the migration apart. Asha trusts the herd to lead the final alignment, and human hands move the grid around their natural rhythm.
chapters.semanticLabel: The reunited herd and a moving city grid align beneath a colossal wheel-shaped storm over the calving ground.

encounters.name: Isobar Roller
encounters.description: A silver cloud-armadillo rolled into a perfect weather ring, with pink lightning tracing pressure lines across its plates as it barrels around the calving pattern.

encounters.name: Monsoon Pinwheel
encounters.description: A riderless six-bladed pinwheel made from rain-frayed storm banners, its long central mast hopping like one leg while the spinning vanes drag wind fronts out of alignment.

finaleFrames.title: Rain on Every Roof
paragraphs: The first calves arrive under a storm bright with their mothers' answering calls. Rain follows the herd outward, filling farm channels, city cisterns, and rivers that children had known only from stories.
Director Sen's relays keep the clinics lit without a chain on any creature. Every settlement that draws power now tends a piece of the open route in return.
finaleFrames.semanticLabel: Newborn thunderbeasts stand in silver rain while rivers brighten from prairie to distant city.

finaleFrames.title: The Herd Leads
paragraphs: Asha began the journey believing leadership meant hiding every doubt and choosing every step. She ends it listening through the soles of her boots as thousands of hooves choose the next season's road.
Boro feels her answer in the ground, and Tavi opens the last old fence ahead. Beneath a living sky, shepherds and citizens travel beside the thunderwild instead of trying to own it.
finaleFrames.semanticLabel: Asha, Boro, and Tavi ride beside the herd through an open fence beneath a renewed storm sky.
